title: Seeds, Origins, Paths, Budgets, and Kill Policy · Runtime Contract: Resource Ceilings

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Bound pages, depth, requests, redirects, wire and decoded bytes, duration, retries, goroutines, open bodies, disk, and report rows.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Resource Ceilings model · Theory Resource Ceilings, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Resource Ceilings precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Resource Ceilings model · Theory Resource Ceilings, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory Go team handles case 6850 during an owner-approved community information audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to admit seeds through owner, origin, path, method, query, budget, kill, and contact policy; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound pages, depth, requests, redirects, wire and decoded bytes, duration, retries, goroutines, open bodies, disk, and report rows.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Resource Ceilings model · Theory Resource Ceilings, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Per-request delay prevents every runaway resource failure.

checks.description: The cumulative go portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: crawler-go-resource-ceilings-7rm97w.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-go-resource-ceilings-7rm97w(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_crawler_go_resource_ceilings_7rm97w\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?candidatePaths < 14)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?admittedPaths < 5 \|\| admittedPaths > candidatePaths)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?stopCases < 5)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?scopeEscapes > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using bound and pages plus specific case evidence.
